evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports both modern runtime access (`initializeSupabase`, `getSupabaseClient`) and a deprecated compatibility proxy (`export const supabase = createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()`), keeping two architectural paths active in the same module.

`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/utils.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, and `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` are compatibility/barrel facades with high importer counts, which centralize traffic but diffuse clear ownership boundaries.

Global event coupling is spread across feature hooks (`useSettingsModal` listens to `openSettings`; `useMediaGalleryHandlers` listens to `selectShotForAddition`) instead of explicit parent-child orchestration, making control flow non-local.

Cross-cutting browser-global effects are embedded in feature-level hooks/components (`window.history.replaceState` in `useSettingsModal`; document/window listeners and DOM class/scroll manipulation in `useMobileInteractions` and `LightboxShell`), increasing subsystem coupling.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts builds a memoized object that only re-lists incoming fields without translation/validation/behavior (lines 38-93), while consumers in src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx just spread those props into control components (lines 226-228).

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts defines a very large parameter bag and composes four partial builders into one state object (lines 14-233), then memoizes with dependency [params] (lines 236-240), but src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts passes a freshly built object each render (lines 553-555), making that memo effectively ceremonial.

Lightbox/edit flows layer multiple wrapper models (environment/shared/edit/render) before final rendering in src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx (lines 95-131), increasing indirection cost for simple UI prop flow.

Task metadata handoff is spread across multiple envelope shapes (`orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, `individual_segment_params`) with repeated fallback chains in src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx (lines 94-140, 152-201).

Producer-side payload building writes overlapping representations in src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts (top-level plus orchestration details, lines 116-178, 216-238) and src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts (state/orchestrator reconstruction and stripping, lines 157-186, 315+), while server-side completion logic repeats multi-path extraction/fallback in supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts (lines 107-115, 192-243) and supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts (lines 74-81).
This seam design forces each integration point to know historical payload variants instead of a single normalized contract.

Multiple assigned files are migration shims that are only pass-through re-exports (for example `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx:1-5`, `src/shared/hooks/useBatchImageLoading.ts:1-5`, `src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts:1-5`), increasing low-level indirection without local behavior.

Several deprecated shim files do not include explicit sunset metadata (`@deprecationOwner`/`@removalTarget`) unlike better-specified cases such as `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts:3-5` and `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:86-98`, making cleanup mechanics inconsistent at function/file level.
`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:3` imports `ConfirmDialog` via a deprecated compatibility path, and `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx:1-5` adds another forwarding hop.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:55-83` keeps a dynamic proxy compatibility surface with repeated runtime casts and traps plus sentinel `void` references (`:99-100`), indicating migration scaffolding still embedded in core runtime access.

Error propagation style is mixed across core paths: `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` throws wrapped `Error` in some branches (lines 48, 98, 134, 157) but throws raw `variantError` in another branch (line 116).

`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts` logs via `handleError(...)` but then rethrows original `unknown` errors (lines 441-443, 522-527), so downstream callers may receive heterogeneous error types.

Some flows convert failures to values instead of exceptions: `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/MyLorasTab/hooks/useLoraSubmission.ts` returns `null` after `toast.error(...)` on upload failure (lines 160-163, 180-183), while sibling task/data hooks throw on failure (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` line 69; `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` lines 48, 98).

Multiple silent catches suppress failures without consistent logging/typing: `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts` ignores localStorage exceptions (lines 9-13), and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx` swallows pointer-capture errors (lines 166, 231).

Server-side response behavior is inconsistent: `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` returns raw `error.message` to clients in catch paths (lines 468, 497), while sibling completion logic generally throws internal errors upward (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` lines 73-77, 217-221; `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` lines 156-161).

findings.summary: Feature behavior depends on global app events instead of explicit local interfaces, creating non-obvious coupling across modules.
evidence: `useSettingsModal` subscribes to `'openSettings'` via `useAppEventListener` and mutates modal state from external emitters.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` subscribes to `'selectShotForAddition'`, allowing external modules to trigger shot changes without direct dependency wiring.
findings.suggestion: Replace global event subscriptions for these flows with explicit command props or domain-scoped controller hooks (e.g., settings controller, gallery selection controller) so trigger sources are visible at composition boundaries.

findings.summary: Multiple high-traffic compatibility facades keep legacy and new paths alive simultaneously, weakening ownership clarity.
evidence: `supabase/client.ts` maintains a deprecated proxy export (`supabase`) alongside new runtime APIs, with lazy fallback initialization and deprecation metadata in the same file.
`useMobile.tsx` is a deprecated compatibility re-export surface still serving many callers.
`handleError.ts` retains deprecated alias exports (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) in a core error hub.
`generationAndShots.ts` is a canonical type barrel with very broad import reach, increasing central coupling pressure.
findings.suggestion: Execute a bounded migration plan: remove deprecated exports behind measurable gates, split legacy adapters into dedicated `compat/` modules, and reduce central barrel usage by importing domain types/functions from their owning modules where practical.

findings.summary: Browser-global state/listener management is scattered across feature hooks, making lifecycle ownership hard to reason about.
evidence: `useSettingsModal` performs `window.history.replaceState` directly during location-state handling.
`useMobileInteractions` attaches/removes global `window`/`document` listeners for scroll and outside click.
`LightboxShell` mutates global document/html state (`classList`, focus/blur behavior, scroll restoration, `focusout` listener) as part of feature rendering.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared browser-effect policies into dedicated platform lifecycle utilities (e.g., history-state coordinator, global listener manager, scroll-lock service) and consume them through narrow hooks so global behavior has single ownership.

findings.summary: Button-group hook is a pass-through abstraction that adds indirection without behavior.
evidence: useButtonGroupProps manually copies each field from input to output and only memoizes the copied object (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts:38-93).
Layout consumers spread these grouped props directly into presentational controls without additional semantics (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx:226-228).
findings.suggestion: Remove `useButtonGroupProps` and pass strongly typed group objects directly from render-model hooks; keep grouping at the callsite where behavior decisions are made, not in a dedicated copy-only hook.

findings.summary: Image edit state assembly uses a massive parameter bag and ineffective memo boundary.
evidence: `useImageEditValue` accepts a very large all-fields interface and reconstructs the same shape via segmented builders (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:14-233).

Memoization depends on the entire `params` object identity (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:236-240), while caller builds a fresh object inline each render (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:553-555), so recomputation is effectively guaranteed.
findings.suggestion: Split the image edit context into smaller stable contexts/slices (mode, canvas, generation forms) and memoize by stable primitive/function dependencies rather than a single composite object identity.

findings.summary: Client and server integration seams rely on multi-shape payload fallback chains instead of one normalized task contract.
evidence: UI details resolution repeatedly checks `individual_segment_params`, `orchestrator_details`, and `full_orchestrator_payload` for the same concepts (src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx:94-140, 152-201).

Payload builder writes and then cleans legacy/parallel fields (src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:116-178, 216-238), while server completion logic repeats different extraction paths for run/task IDs and counts (supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:107-115, 192-243; supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:74-81).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared canonical task param schema and normalize once at task creation; server/UI should consume only normalized fields, with legacy path migration isolated to a single adapter.

findings.summary: Shot image callback hook blends unrelated concerns and uses fragile string-key cache predicates.
evidence: Single hook handles DB deletion, sibling discovery, optimistic cache mutation, query invalidation, and UI-level adapters in one flow (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:36-120, 122-239).
Cache updates depend on raw key prefix string checks (`query.queryKey[0] === 'segment-child-generations'`) at multiple points (lines 95, 105), which is brittle compared with typed query-key helpers.
findings.suggestion: Extract deletion/repository logic into a dedicated service hook, keep UI adapters thin, and replace string-prefix predicates with centralized query key builders/selectors.

findings.summary: Shot image upload seam exposes incompatible input contracts across shared handler types and manager props.
evidence: `ImageUploadHandler` is defined as `(event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => Promise<void>` in `imageHandlers.ts`.
`ShotUploadProps.onImageUpload` and `BaseShotImageManagerProps.onImageUpload` are both `(files: File[]) => Promise<void>` in `ShotImageManager/types.ts`.
Both types are used as the same cross-component API surface for shot image operations, forcing adapters and increasing mismatch risk.
findings.suggestion: Unify on a single upload boundary type (prefer `File[]` at shared domain seam), and keep event-to-files extraction inside UI-only components. Update `ShotImageHandlers.onUpload` and related consumers to one canonical signature.

findings.summary: Primary task resolution and generation transformation disagree on accepted `tasks` field shape for the same table contract.
evidence: `parsePrimaryTaskId` in `generationTaskRepository.ts` returns `invalid_tasks_shape` when `tasks` is not an array of strings.
`extractTaskId` in `generationTransformers.ts` accepts both `string` and `string[]` as valid and returns the first task id either way.
`generationTaskCache.ts` depends on repository mapping status; mixed parsing rules can produce null task mappings in one path while other UI paths still derive task IDs.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical parser for `generations.tasks` (including legacy string support if still needed) and reuse it from repository, cache, and transformer layers so task-id handoff behavior is identical.

findings.summary: Two travel-generation request builders emit different motion payload fields for equivalent basic-mode scenarios.
evidence: `buildTaskParams` calls `buildMotionTaskFields` with `omitBasicPhaseConfig: true`, intentionally omitting `phase_config` in basic mode unless preset/advanced is active.
`buildTravelRequestBodyV2` calls `buildMotionTaskFields` without `omitBasicPhaseConfig`, so default behavior includes `phase_config` even for basic mode.
Both modules construct task request bodies for travel-between-images flows, creating boundary inconsistency in payload semantics.
findings.suggestion: Centralize motion payload policy in one shared function with explicit mode rules, and have both request-building paths call that same policy so `phase_config` inclusion is deterministic across integrations.

findings.summary: Many compatibility shim files are deprecated but lack enforceable sunset metadata, leaving migration debt open-ended.
evidence: `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx:1-5` and `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx:1-5` mark deprecated paths but do not define owner/removal target tags.
`src/shared/hooks/useBatchImageLoading.ts:1-5`, `src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts:1-5`, and `src/shared/lib/deepEqual.ts:1-9` are pass-through deprecated shims without explicit removal metadata.
By contrast, `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts:3-5` includes `@deprecationOwner` and `@removalTarget`, showing inconsistency in migration governance at file level.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all deprecated shim headers to include `@deprecationOwner` and `@removalTarget`, then track them with a single migration checklist and remove shims in priority order by import count.

findings.summary: Active hook logic still imports through a deprecated shim path, preserving unnecessary indirection in runtime code.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:3` imports `ConfirmDialog` from `@/shared/components/ConfirmDialog`.
`src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx:2-5` explicitly marks that module as deprecated and re-exports from `@/shared/components/dialogs/ConfirmDialog`.
findings.suggestion: Update `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` to import directly from `@/shared/components/dialogs/ConfirmDialog` and then remove or quarantine the deprecated shim to legacy-only callers.

findings.summary: Compatibility shims use mixed export forms (`export *`, named, default), which makes low-level API exposure and cleanup behavior unpredictable.
evidence: `src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx:5`, `src/shared/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts:5`, and `src/shared/lib/NetworkStatusManager.ts:5` use `export *` passthroughs that can silently widen surface area.

`src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx:5` uses a named re-export and `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx:5` uses default re-export, creating mixed migration semantics across similar shim modules.
findings.suggestion: Use a single shim policy: avoid `export *` for deprecated bridges, re-export explicit symbols only, and align all shim modules to one consistent export style to reduce accidental API drift during migration.

findings.summary: Domain-specific API token workflow is stored in generic shared hook package
evidence: `useApiTokens` performs user auth/session checks and directly manages token persistence/RPCs (`user_api_tokens`, `generate-pat`, `revoke-pat`).

Neighbor hooks in the same package are infrastructure-style abstractions (`useAsyncOperation`, `useAutoSaveSettings`) with reusable cross-feature behavior, creating mixed ownership semantics within one directory.
findings.suggestion: Move `useApiTokens` into a feature/domain-owned location (for example an account/settings or billing/token module), keep `shared/hooks` for cross-domain primitives, and expose only a thin public import path if needed for compatibility.

findings.summary: Test files use inconsistent placement patterns across sibling packages
evidence: Hooks follow a dedicated `__tests__` directory convention under `shared/hooks`.
Components place tests beside source files at `shared/components` root, yielding two competing package-level test organization patterns in nearby shared modules.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one repository-wide convention for test location per package type (either colocated or `__tests__`) and migrate outliers so path patterns remain predictable for future ownership and maintenance work.

findings.summary: Deprecation/removal contracts are documented but not enforceable in code paths that remain active.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` defines `LEGACY_SUPABASE_PROXY_REMOVE_BY`/`LEGACY_SUPABASE_PROXY_OWNER` then only references them with `void` statements.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` marks `reportRuntimeError` and `RuntimeErrorOptions` deprecated with `@removalTarget 2026-06-30` but still exports them without any guard.
`README.md` frames strict migration gates, but reviewed compatibility exports have no local mechanism that blocks new usage or fails builds on overdue removals.
findings.suggestion: Add machine-enforced governance checks: an ESLint `no-restricted-imports` rule for deprecated entry points, a CI date-based deprecation check, and removal gates that fail when deprecated exports remain after target date.

findings.summary: Supabase runtime contract remains ambiguous because both explicit bootstrap and implicit lazy fallback are first-class behaviors.
evidence: `README.md` states `initializeSupabase()` is the explicit runtime contract and bootstrap initializes once.
`getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime-access errors and automatically calls `initializeSupabase()` as fallback, so callers can bypass bootstrap ordering without immediate failure.
The deprecated `supabase` proxy remains exported and forwards all property access, preserving implicit behavior at runtime.
findings.suggestion: Tighten the contract in phases: keep fallback only behind a dev/test flag, emit hard telemetry on fallback use, and then fail fast in production once migration is complete; remove proxy export behind a CI usage gate.

findings.summary: Governance metadata is scattered as ad-hoc comments/constants rather than a centralized contract registry.
evidence: Contract hints (`@publicContract`, deprecation tags, owner/remove-by constants) are embedded per-file with inconsistent mechanisms.
`src/types/generationAndShots.ts` is described as canonical barrel but has no adjacent machine-readable ownership/migration metadata, unlike the deprecation-heavy runtime files.
This distribution increases the chance that policy updates happen in one module but not others.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single governance manifest (or typed contract helper) that records ownership, deprecation targets, and removal gates, then consume it in lint/CI checks and reference it from module docs/comments.

findings.summary: Identical mutation/task flows emit different error shapes (wrapped Error, raw Supabase error, raw unknown), making failure handling non-uniform.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations` throws `new Error(...)` for most DB failures but throws raw `variantError` at line 116.
`createImageGenerationTask` and `createBatchImageGenerationTasks` call `handleError(...)` then `throw error` (original unknown) at lines 441-443 and 522-527.
The shared facade already provides a normalized path (`handleAndRethrow`) in `handleError.ts` line 58.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one propagation contract (prefer normalized `AppError`) and replace raw rethrows with `handleAndRethrow(...)` or `throw handleError(...)` so callers always receive the same error type and metadata.

findings.summary: Failure semantics are split between exception throwing and `null` returns, forcing callers to guess which contract applies.
evidence: `useLoraSubmission` upload branches emit `toast.error(...)` then `return null` on failure (lines 160-163, 180-183).
Sibling task/mutation hooks throw on failure (`useGenerationMutations` lines 48, 98, 134; `useTasks` line 69).
This creates mixed caller responsibilities: null-checking in some paths versus catch/onError handling in others.
findings.suggestion: Unify async failure contracts per layer: either throw typed errors consistently (preferred for hooks/services) or adopt an explicit Result object everywhere; avoid mixing nullable-error and exception styles in adjacent APIs.

findings.summary: Several catch blocks intentionally swallow errors without structured reporting, creating hidden failure modes.
evidence: `useDiscordSignIn` catches localStorage errors and ignores them completely (`catch {}` at lines 11-13).
`StrokeOverlay` silently ignores pointer-capture/release failures (`catch { /* ok */ }` at lines 166 and 231).
The project has a centralized non-toast reporting path via `handleError(..., { showToast: false })` but these catches bypass it.
findings.suggestion: At minimum, send suppressed exceptions to centralized logging with `handleError(..., { showToast: false, context: ... })` so operational visibility is preserved while keeping UI behavior unchanged.

findings.summary: Edge functions use inconsistent error exposure and wrapping, including returning raw internal messages in some paths.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` returns interpolated backend errors directly (`Failed to record cost in ledger: ${ledgerError.message}` at line 468; catch response uses `error?.message` at line 497).
`orchestrator.ts` and `generation.ts` build internal-detail errors and throw upward (e.g., lines 73-77, 217-221, 156-161), yielding a different exposure pattern.
No shared edge-level error formatter is used across these sibling modules.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a single edge error policy: sanitize client-facing messages, log full internals server-side, and use a shared error response helper to enforce consistent status/message/context behavior across functions.
